resources.string: Free, open-source sound amplification for any earbuds. No accounts, no ads — nothing leaves your phone.

OpenHearing is a sound-amplification and hearing-assistance tool. It is NOT a medical device and NOT a substitute for a professional hearing exam. It does not diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any condition. If you have concerns about your hearing, please see an audiologist or doctor. Always keep the volume at a comfortable level and stop if you feel any discomfort.
Amplifying sound around you. Tap Stop to turn off.
Please read and acknowledge the above before using OpenHearing.

Estimate only — uncalibrated. This check is not a medical test and not a substitute for a professional hearing exam. Use headphones in a quiet room. Keep the volume comfortable; stop if anything is too loud.
You\'ll hear quiet tones at different pitches in each ear. After each tone, tap whether you heard it. It takes a couple of minutes.
Each point is the quietest level you heard at that pitch. Lower points mean quieter sounds were heard. Detailed values are listed below.
Chart of hearing check results. Detailed values are listed in the table below.
These are uncalibrated estimates, not a diagnosis. For a professional hearing assessment, see an audiologist.
Amplifies sound around you in real time. Output is hard-limited for safety, but keep the level comfortable and stop if anything is too loud. Not a medical device.
Run the hearing check first so assist mode can use your sound profile.
Stop assist to change the amplification level.
Sets the maximum loudness assist mode will ever reach. Tap Preview, then lower the level if the tone is too loud. (For a precise dB SPL calibration you\'d need a sound-level meter — see docs.)
Free software under the GNU GPL v3. No accounts, no ads, no trackers — the app never asks for internet access, so nothing you do can leave your phone.
